description_long: This is a modern, tactical-style axe with a single-piece construction, likely forged from metal. The handle is straight with a slight ergonomic curve and features three circular cutouts, possibly for weight reduction or grip enhancement. The blade is broad and wedge-shaped, with a textured upper surface and a sharp, straight cutting edge. The poll (rear) of the axe head is angular and tapers to a point, suggesting potential use for breaching or prying. The entire object has a matte, dark metallic finish, and there are no visible wooden or composite elements. The design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative markings or branding.
description: A modern, all-metal tactical axe with a broad wedge blade, ergonomic handle, and three circular grip holes. The surface is matte and dark, with a textured blade head and angular poll.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the axe, including the broad blade, textured head, and three circular holes in the handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view showing the thinness of the blade and handle, with minimal visible detail except the narrow profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the same features visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite direction of view 1, again highlighting the thin profile and straight alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, all-metal handle with a slight ergonomic curve, three circular cutouts for grip, and a flat cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, wedge-shaped single blade with a straight cutting edge, textured upper surface, and angular poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Single-piece forged construction; blade and handle are one continuous piece of metal.
